# requestAuthorization()

**Instance Method**

Prompts a person to give FinanceKit authorization to access financial data.

## Declaration

```swift
func requestAuthorization() async throws -> AuthorizationStatus
```

## Return Value

An [doc://com.apple.financekit/documentation/FinanceKit/AuthorizationStatus] value that indicates the current state of authorization.

## Discussion

If there are no accounts are available to display, the framework presents a “No Accounts” screen and returns a status of [doc://com.apple.financekit/documentation/FinanceKit/AuthorizationStatus/authorized] or [doc://com.apple.financekit/documentation/FinanceKit/AuthorizationStatus/denied] depending on the state of a person’s consent.

It’s safe to call this method multiple times; the framework prompts a person only if necessary.

## Authorization

- **authorizationStatus()**: Checks the authorization status for the calling application.
- **AuthorizationStatus**
